label: RAG CHATBOT
title: Chat With My Work
description: An in-site retrieval-augmented (RAG) chatbot grounded in a curated, structured knowledge base of my work history and projects. Each response cites the exact source chunk it was generated from. The system is designed to avoid speculation and will decline to answer when the knowledge base doesn’t support a claim. I built it to deepen my understanding of RAG architecture and the practical considerations of deploying it in a production setting. AMAIA (Ask My AI Anything!)

meta.proudOf: Making the portfolio interactive — you can ask questions and see grounded, cited answers
meta.learned: Operational details matter: logging, citations, and knowledge hygiene make or break trust

chatbot.title: Chat with Dan
chatbot.description: Ask about roles, projects, and outcomes. Answers are grounded in a knowledge base and include sources.

sections.label: INTRO
sections.heading: What you’re looking at
sections.content:
This is an in-site retrieval-augmented chatbot for my portfolio website.

It’s grounded in a curated, structured knowledge base of my work history and projects. Responses include citations to the exact source chunks used to generate the answer.

**Placeholder:** After you try it, I’ll refine this section to explain the mental model, the constraints (what it will and won’t answer), and what I learned building it.

sections.label: EVOLUTION
sections.heading: How it evolved
sections.content:
**Placeholder:** A short narrative of the evolution goes here.

- Started with a simple question-answer loop against a small set of documents.
- Added chunking + embeddings so retrieval is reliable and explainable.
- Introduced source citations so users can verify claims.
- Layered in guardrails (decline when unsupported, avoid speculation).
- Built an admin view to inspect sessions, prompts, and failure modes.

I’ll tighten the language and add concrete examples once the final knowledge base is set.

sections.label: QUALITY
sections.heading: Evaluation, tests, and deployment
sections.content:
**Placeholder:** This section will cover how I evaluate the chatbot, what I test, and how I deploy it.

**Evaluation approach**
- Define a small benchmark set of representative questions.
- Track citation quality (are sources relevant and sufficient?).
- Measure refusal correctness (decline when the KB doesn’t support an answer).

**Tests**
- Unit tests for chunking + retrieval wiring.
- Contract tests for the API (including SSE streaming).
- Regression tests for previously-good answers.

**Deployment**
- CI checks + build validation.
- Observability for latency, token/cost, and error rates.
- A simple rollback story if a model/config change regresses quality.
